overnight_news: **Iran expands strikes across Middle East, escalating tensions**: Oil spiked to $81 Brent/$74 WTI (moderated by Trump guarantees for Strait of Hormuz shipping); impacts energy/inflation-sensitive stocks like DJI, SPX, COMP.[1]
**Oil markets on edge from Iran moves to restrict Strait of Hormuz**: Fuels inflation fears, delays Fed rate cut expectations to September; pressures cyclicals.[1]
**Defence stocks repriced amid conflict (e.g., Northrop Grumman NOC)**: Investors eye strategic programs, procurement pace; NOC in focus for modernization funding.[3]
**BAE Systems (BA.L) hits 52-week high**: £83.6B backlog, AUKUS/European defence spending signals; up 6.11% on risk-off rotation.[3]
**AeroVironment (AVAV) volatile on $1.4B Space Force contract reopen**: Surged 18% then fell 17%; highlights procurement risks in defence theme.[3]
